meta.scenario: deep house B to A relative, BPM decrease 123 to 118, intro exit, T2 skips its own long intro
meta.source: Kerri Chandler b2b Jeremy Underground @ Boiler Room 2016-08-18

transition_arc: 12B (E major) to 12A (D minor) — relative minor move. BPM decreases (123 to 118), cool-down. T1 exits from its low-energy intro section (bar 48). T2 has a 16-bar intro the DJ skips (from_bar=16) — entering at first real musical phrase. Relative key is smooth; BPM drop reinforces introspective deeper feel.

annotated_actions.t1_state: T1 bar 0: bright E major groove, piano + kick + bass, RMS 0.48
T1 bar 48: section=INTRO-OUTRO, drums=0.15, harmonic=0.20, rms=0.18 — only filtered piano and faint kick
annotated_actions.t2_state: T2 bar 16: first real musical content after 16-bar near-silent intro
annotated_actions.why: T1 at bar 48 is in its sparsest section. Low energy exit matches the cool-down intention.

annotated_actions.t2_state: T2 bar 16: first kick hit, pads beginning to filter in, bass=0.0
annotated_actions.why: T2 from_bar=16: skips T2's 16-bar near-silent synth intro. other:0.6 brings T2 D minor pads — relative key means E major and D minor share the same notes.

annotated_actions.t1_state: T1 bar 56: bass=0.05, drums=0.08 — nearly inaudible
annotated_actions.why: T1 bass essentially gone. Swap is clean — formalizing the bass transfer already happening naturally.

annotated_actions.t2_state: T2 bar 32: full deep house groove at 117.5 BPM, D minor, bass+kick+pads, RMS 0.45
annotated_actions.why: T2 full groove. BPM decrease (123 to 118) and key shift (major to relative minor) achieves the intended deeper feel.
